title: Vegetable oil supply per person vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: Average vegetable oil supply per capita, measured in kilograms per year versus gross domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Vegetable oil supply per person vs. GDP per capita

Average vegetable oil supply per capita, measured in kilograms per year versus gross domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

This scatter plot maps the relationship between vegetable oil supply and economic output for various countries in 2023, with the horizontal axis representing GDP per capita on a logarithmic scale and the vertical axis showing vegetable oil supply in kilograms per year per capita. The data points are color-coded by region, distinguishing North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania. A broad cluster of nations, including many in Africa such as Niger, Burkina Faso, and DR Congo, as well as Asian countries like Afghanistan and Cambodia, occupies the lower-left quadrant, characterized by GDP levels below $10,000 and vegetable oil supplies generally under 10 kg. Moving toward the center of the chart, countries like Brazil, Algeria, and Libya display supplies between 15 and 25 kg with GDPs ranging from roughly $10,000 to $20,000. In the upper-right section, high-income nations including the United States, Canada, and Australia show supplies between 20 and 30 kg alongside GDPs exceeding $50,000. Notably, several European countries such as Denmark and Norway appear in the far right with GDPs approaching or exceeding $100,000 but maintain vegetable oil supplies in the lower range of 5 to 10 kg. Conversely, Bulgaria and Austria sit higher on the supply axis, between 20 and 30 kg, with GDPs around $40,000 to $60,000. The distribution reveals no single linear trend; instead, supply levels vary significantly across similar income brackets, with some lower-income nations like Guinea-Bissau reaching 20 kg while wealthier nations like Iceland remain near 10 kg. All monetary values are expressed in international dollars at 2021 prices.
